name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: I joined Morning Joe yesterday to speak about HR 3233, which creates a bipartisan, independent commission to investigate January 6, the worst attack on our nation’s Capitol & an attack incited by the former president.  https://t.co/aP3YIw3TbS

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: Without it, our country won’t be able to heal. This commission will help. Today’s vote was a step in the right direction. The next one is ensuring we get this bill to the president’s desk to be signed into law.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: The attacks of January 6 are a symptom of a much deeper problem and the House Republicans who voted against this commission are a part of it. The American people deserve a comprehensive accounting of the threat & a plan that addresses how we as a nation can move forward.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: I stood in the House gallery as the mob broke into the Capitol building. There’s no doubt their intent was to undermine our democracy and do harm to those who would uphold it.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: It’s a sad day when these Members can’t even stand to support an independent, bipartisan commission focused on the truth.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: Today, the House passed H.R. 3233 to establish a commission to address the attack on the Capitol on 1/6 and how we got there as a nation. The vote was bipartisan, but far too many of my Republican colleagues still opposed it.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: As a veteran, former federal prosecutor, & now Congresswoman, I’ve taken the oath of office many times.   Each time it’s meaningful & powerful.   Opposing a bipartisan, independent commission to examine the events of January 6th betrays the commitments we make in that oath.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: Today, I rose on the House floor to speak about why it’s so crucial we establish an independent, bipartisan commission to examine the attack on the Capitol on January 6th.  https://t.co/85w7oxNfl5

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: It also is the perfect example of why we need to establish this commission & take an unflinching look at that day, investigate the security failures & what led to this act of domestic terrorism.   Without it, we won’t be able to move forward as a nation.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: House Republicans ousted Liz Cheney from leadership partly because she refused to sweep the events of 1/6 under the rug.   Their attempt to whitewash an attack on our democracy and prioritize loyalty to Donald Trump’s “Big Lie” over loyalty to the nation is a disgrace.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: Later today, the House will be voting on H.R. 3233, which would create a *bipartisan*, *independent* commission to examine the attack on the Capitol on 1/6.   I strongly urge my colleagues to vote in favor of this legislation for the sake of our nation.

tweet: As we look towards economic recovery after the pandemic, we need to focus on how we’re building an economy that works for working women.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: Patrick shouldn’t have had to fight the VA for care. And neither should any of our veterans. It’s time to pass H.R. 8261 and ensure that we uphold our commitment to our service members.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: I’m thinking of the tragic loss of New Jersey's Patrick Duva, who died last week.  “His family said VA medical staff told Duva that his cancer was related to the toxic exposure he experienced during his military service, but VA ‘wouldn't document it.’”   https://t.co/NB94y6IbMi

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: That’s why I’m proud to co-sponsor @RepRaulRuizMD’s bill, H.R. 8261, the Presumptive Benefits for War Fights Exposed to Burn Pits & Other Toxins Act, which would strengthen benefits for veterans suffering from burn pit related illnesses and est. presumption for overseas service.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: But that still leaves an important issue unresolved: presumption. Just like with the 9/11 VCF, we need to ensure that any veteran who developed these health issues and served near burn pits is guaranteed care.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: And the House has passed my amendment this summer to increase funding for research at the Airborne Hazards and Burn Pits Center of Excellence in East Orange, NJ.  https://t.co/7yBeoaH2hj

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: 2️⃣ It directs DoD to provide to the VA and Congress a list of all locations where DoD used burn pits, for the purposes of research, healthcare delivery, and disability compensation.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: Last year, the National Defense Authorization Act included 2 provisions to address burn pits:  1️⃣It requires any periodic health assessments for service members to include an evaluation of whether they were stationed near burn pits and/or were exposed to toxic airborne chemicals;

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: One of the first meetings I ever had in Congress was with @Burnpit360, Jon Stewart, and John Feal to talk about how we can do better and learn from the 9/11 VCF and their work to help those exposed to the toxic fumes from Ground Zero. I promised we would not let this go.  https://t.co/D4yePHN52u

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: Burn pits have been called the Agent Orange of our generation. And that’s what it is looking like unless we stand up for our veterans and demand better.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: In fact, as of September of this year, the VA denied 78 PERCENT of disability claims from burn pits:  https://t.co/FfqPPYbYdI

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: The VA has a Burn Pits Registry to document exposure and health concerns, but still maintains that research “does not show evidence of long-term health problems from exposure to burn pits.”   https://t.co/8keS89Fw6d

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: Watch this CBS piece for an important overview of the use of burn pits and the impact on our veterans:  https://t.co/rFF0FFJIeY

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: Too many of our veterans have been exposed to harmful chemicals from burn pits, which we know lead to increased risk of lung and respiratory diseases.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: Burn pits were used in Iraq and Afghanistan to get rid of waste — and by waste, I mean everything — from batteries, to plastics, to jet fuel. And as of last year, nine were still in use across the Middle East.

name: Rep. Mikie Sherrill
tweet: 🧵 This #VeteransDay, I want to renew the call for comprehensive action to address burn pits and the serious, lasting health impact their use has had on a generation of veterans.
